tagline: Claude prompting, but more literal — spell out structure; it infers less than Opus.
effort_line: output_config.effort (low|medium|high|xhigh|max) is the primary dial, paired with adaptive thinking. On Opus 4.8 thinking is OFF unless you set thinking:{type:'adaptive'}; manual budget_tokens returns 400 (thinking:{type:'disabled'} is still accepted and equals omitting).
idioms: Front-load a complete spec in one turn: long documents at the TOP, query/instructions/examples at the END (up to ~30% quality gain on complex multi-doc inputs); wrap docs in <document>/<document_content>/<source>.

State scope explicitly — it's literal and won't generalize an instruction across items or infer unmade requests ('apply this to every section, not just the first'); give the reason behind a rule ('do X because Y') so it generalizes from the explanation.
guardrail_extra: Don't use aggressive 'CRITICAL / you MUST / ALWAYS / if in doubt use X' tool-forcing — on 4.5/4.6+ (and the even-more-literal 4.7/4.8) it overtriggers tools/skills and overthinks; downgrade to plain 'Use this tool when...'.
verify_note: Code-review harnesses regressed because 4.8 obeys 'only high-severity / be conservative' faithfully — instead instruct 'report every finding with confidence + severity; filter downstream.'
notes: Flagship of the 2026 line (Fable 5, Mythos 5, Opus 4.8/4.7/4.6/4.5, Sonnet 5/4.6, Haiku 4.5). 1M-token default context. The 'start at xhigh' advice is Opus-4.7/4.8-specific — Sonnet 5 defaults to high, Fable 5 starts at high; don't read it as universal. Sonnet 5 differs: adaptive thinking ON by default, a new tokenizer emits ~30% more tokens (retune max_tokens), non-default sampling 400. Haiku 4.5 is NOT in the effort/adaptive family — it still uses manual budget_tokens. Thinking display defaults to 'omitted' on newest models; set thinking.display:'summarized' to see reasoning. For noisy long-context corpora, ask it to extract relevant quotes into <quotes> first, then answer. Opus 4.8 has a persistent cream/serif frontend 'house style' — override it explicitly. Sonnet-specific: it is faster and more literal than Opus — it follows explicit structure well but infers less, so spell out steps and success criteria a touch more, and don't assume it will fill gaps the way Opus does.
